clauses.rationale: The clause only identifies the parties and rental unit; none of the candidate rules apply.
The fixed-term tenancy dates do not concern or conflict with any of the candidate rules.
The clause only states the monthly rent amount and payment date; it does not conflict with any provided rule.
The clause requires a last-month rent deposit of one month's rent and applies it only to the final rental period, consistent with ON-DEPOSIT-CAP and ON-NO-EXTRA-DEPOSITS.
The clause requires a security and damage deposit, which is expressly prohibited by ON-NO-EXTRA-DEPOSITS.
The clause requires a non-refundable pet deposit, which is expressly prohibited by ON-NO-EXTRA-DEPOSITS.
The clause expressly prohibits all pets and animals in the unit and on the property, violating ON-NO-PET-BAN.
The clause permits only a refundable key deposit and caps it at the actual replacement cost of the keys, which is consistent with ON-NO-EXTRA-DEPOSITS.
The clause requires landlord permission for overnight guests and imposes a per-night guest fee, both prohibited by ON-NO-GUEST-BAN.

The clause permits entry at any time without prior notice for inspections or maintenance, contrary to ON-ENTRY-NOTICE, which generally requires at least 24 hours’ written notice and entry between 8 a.m. and 8 p.m.
The clause concerns the tenant's responsibility for cleanliness and does not impose a cleaning fee or otherwise conflict with any candidate rule.
The clause assigns responsibility for repairing tenant- or guest-caused damage but does not require a prohibited deposit or otherwise conflict with any candidate rule.
The $75 administration charge exceeds the $20 cap under ON-NSF-CAP; only separate charges imposed by the landlord's bank may be added.
The clause requires the tenant to provide post-dated cheques, which is prohibited by ON-NO-POSTDATED; this payment method may only be chosen voluntarily.

The clause allows rent increases at any time with only 30 days' notice, contrary to ON-RENT-INCREASE-NOTICE, which requires at least 90 days' written notice and generally permits increases only once every 12 months.
The clause states that no interest will be paid on any deposit, contrary to ON-DEPOSIT-INTEREST, which requires annual interest on the rent deposit.
The clause merely makes contents insurance optional and does not conflict with any of the candidate rules.
The clause imposes an absolute ban on assignment or subletting, contrary to ON-SUBLET-CONSENT, which permits these with landlord consent and prohibits arbitrary or unreasonable refusal.
The clause imposes a general repair and habitability obligation and does not conflict with or trigger any of the provided candidate rules.
The clause violates ON-VITAL-SERVICES because it allows the landlord to deliberately shut off electricity, heat, or water due to late rent.
The clause allows the landlord to change the locks without immediately giving replacement keys to the tenant, contrary to ON-LOCK-CHANGE.
The candidate rules do not address restrictions on smoking, so none apply to this clause.

The clause only requires the tenant to establish and pay for electricity service directly; it does not authorize the landlord to withhold or interfere with a vital service. None of the listed rules prohibit this arrangement.
This severability clause does not conflict with any of the candidate rules; it states that terms conflicting with the legislation are void.
